answer:
I recently needed to argue my point of view about doing chores around our apartment with my roommate. I felt like I was doing the lion's share of cleaning up and taking out the trash while my roommate wasn't pulling their weight. When I brought this up, my roommate got really defensive right away. They denied not doing their fair share and said I was overreacting. 

At first, our conversation escalated into an argument because neither of us was really listening to the other. I think we were both just focused on being "right" instead of understanding each other's perspectives. The more we argued, the more stubborn my roommate became about not needing to do more chores. 

Eventually, I realized that getting angry wasn't going to solve anything. So I took a step back and asked my roommate open-ended questions to try and understand why they felt the way they did. Only then did I realize there was a miscommunication about who was supposed to do what chore and when. Once we talked it through more calmly, we were able to come up with a new system we both agreed was fair.

This experience taught me that jumping into an argumentative mindset right away just makes the other person defensive. It's better to try to sincerely understand their viewpoint first. Asking questions opens up a real dialogue instead of a shouting match. I learned to lead with empathy and patience instead of frustration. In the end, taking the time to see each other's perspective allowed us to resolve the issue in a reasonable way we both felt good about.
title: Describe the last time you needed to argue your point of view. How did the other person respond to your opinion? What did that experience teach you about sharing your thoughts with others?
extData:
{"pronuncFile":"/20230612/b35a2e3e719cc6af75967f1d498895bc.mp3","answerTemplate":"本题模板:

开场陈述:
开始时描述你最近需要争辩观点的一个经历: "The last time I found myself in a position to argue my point of view was during a discussion about [mention the topic, e.g., a work project, a social issue, a personal decision]."

争辩的情境:
描述争辩的背景和你的观点: "The debate arose when [describe the situation or how the topic came up]. My stance on the matter was that [state your point of view]."
强调你为何坚持这个观点: "I was firm in my opinion because [explain the reasoning or beliefs behind your stance]."

对方的反应:
讨论对方如何回应你的观点: "The other person's response to my opinion was [describe their reaction, whether it was understanding, disagreement, surprise]."
描述互动的性质: "The conversation unfolded in a way that was [mention the nature of the interaction, like constructive, heated, respectful]."

从经验中学到的教训:
描述这次经验教会你关于分享思想的事情: "This experience taught me valuable lessons about sharing my thoughts, including [mention lessons learned, such as the importance of listening, the value of different perspectives, the need for clear communication]."
强调适应和学习的重要性: "I learned that in discussions, it's crucial to [discuss adaptations you've made or plan to make, like being more open-minded, finding common ground]."

结论:
总结这次争辩经验的影响: "In conclusion, arguing my point of view in this instance was enlightening and reinforced the significance of [summarize key takeaways, like effective communication, empathy, staying informed]."
结束语: "Engaging in thoughtful discourse, even when opinions differ, is a fundamental aspect of personal growth and understanding in our interactions with others.""}
